records.ety: [L. imputatio an account, a charge: cf. F. imputation.]

defs: The act of imputing or charging; attribution; ascription; also, anything imputed or charged.
quotes: Shylock. Antonio is a good man. Bassanio. Have you heard any imputation to the contrary?
If I had a suit to Master Shallow, I would humor his men with the imputation of being near their master.

defs: Charge or attribution of evil; censure; reproach; insinuation.
quotes: Let us be careful to guard ourselves against these groundless imputation of our enemies.

defs: A setting of something to the account of; the attribution of personal guilt or personal righteousness of another; as, the imputation of the sin of Adam, or the righteousness of Christ.
